excerpt: The month itself, رمضان الحرام, spans thirty days without deficiency or excess. During this period, the believer disciplines the body as an act of obedience.
source_location_hint: On fasting: Chapter on the sacred month

excerpt: The believer thus fasts during the day (the manifest realm) by acquiring no knowledge from the apparent people and sharing none of the inner secrets with them.
The Prophet himself affirmed: Sha'bān shall never reach completion, nor shall Ramaḍān fall short. The reason Sha'bān cannot reach thirty lies in the celebrated Night of the Midpoint.
source_location_hint: On lunar sighting and the fixed calendar

excerpt: Thus, when Allāh ordained counted days, how could the month of Ramaḍān oscillate between twenty-nine and thirty days?
source_location_hint: On the fixed nature of Ramaḍān

excerpt: In reality, the month of Ramaḍān refers to the Successor of the Prophet—the Asās (Foundation), Amīr al-Muʾminīn ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (peace be upon him).
source_location_hint: On the symbolic identity of Ramaḍān as the Foundation

excerpt: The Prophet likewise said: Travel, that you may gain booty. Here the journey means entry into the feast of knowledge, and the spoils signify the discovery of the secrets of existence.
source_location_hint: On spiritual travel and the acquisition of knowledge

excerpt: Of these twelve, the ninth is the Asās: [table of twelve stations]. In the Arabic numerical system, only four degrees are employed—reflecting the four letters and four words of the divine testimony.
source_location_hint: On the hierarchy of the Limits and numerical symbolism

excerpt: In another circle of meaning, the month of Ramaḍān, being the ninth month, stands as the symbolic embodiment of Muḥammad Mustaфā, for the Prophet completed nine dahr (eternal cycles).
source_location_hint: On the Prophet's completion of nine cycles

excerpt: The esoteric interpretation of these thirty days reveals the thirty degrees to which the cosmos and the soul both testify: Six Speakers, Seven Imāms, Twelve Ḥudūd, Five Upper Limits.
source_location_hint: On the thirty symbolic degrees of Ramaḍān

excerpt: Every thirty marks a turning point. After the fasts of Rajab, the twenty-nine-day Sunna fasts of Sha'bān begin. After thirty days of Ramaḍān, the month of Shawwāl commences.
source_location_hint: On the cyclical nature of the months and Imāmate

excerpt: The Messenger of Allāh declared at Ghadīr Khumm: Am I not more worthy of guardianship over you than your own selves? The sun of religion illuminates the spiritual cosmos through divine knowledge.
source_location_hint: On the Prophet as the religious sun

excerpt: Just as the moon's light increases with each passing night, so the Foundation's call gradually gains strength, its authority becoming ever more manifest. When the moon reaches full luminescence, all benefit equally from its radiance.
source_location_hint: On the gradual manifestation of the Asās's authority

excerpt: Just as the people of knowledge are obligated to fast upon perceiving the moon's separation from the sun, so true believers must obey ʿAlī from the moment the Prophet hinted at his succession.
source_location_hint: On the obligation to follow the Asās

excerpt: Should a person reveal the exalted rank of one of these thirty Imāms in the world of taqiyya to the hostile and deceived, his expiation becomes: he must henceforth acquire knowledge of sixty Imāms for each single Imām whose station he has unveiled.
source_location_hint: On the expiation for breaking the spiritual fast

excerpt: As a synthesis of all these mysteries, the Holy Prophet taught that none shall recognize the Qāʾim save through five Limits: the Asās, the Imām, the Bāb, the Hujjat, and the Dāʿī.
source_location_hint: On the five Limits through which the Qāʾim is recognized
